question_text: A car engine converts chemical energy from petrol. Which statement about energy conservation is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: All chemical energy in petrol becomes kinetic energy to move the car

options.label: B
options.text: Total energy is conserved but some is converted to heat and sound rather than useful motion

options.label: C
options.text: Some energy is destroyed in the engine to reduce pollution

options.label: D
options.text: Energy conservation only applies to electrical devices, not engines

explanation: Energy is always conserved, but car engines are inefficient. Much of the chemical energy from petrol is converted to heat (through the radiator) and sound, with only a fraction becoming kinetic energy to move the car.
